title: Organoid well plate scanning
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"10fgf","text":"This is a conditional or 'smart' imaging routine written using the JOBs module within Nikon's NIS elements software (version 6.02.03). The JOB is designed to serially scan each well within a well-plate at 4x magnification and identify the XY position of organoids in each well. The program will then loop over those XY positions, identifying the top and bottom Z-stack positions for each organoid and then perform a Z-stack at 20x magnification. e97ce4a93fb6a1e4782c6f4e98"}}}}
steps: Create optical configurations (OCs) for the fluorophores you wish to image. For this JOB you will need an OC linked to a 4x objective and and OC linked to a 20x air objective.
Using the joystick move the stage so that the objective is beneath the first well to be imaged (usually top left corner of the well plate).
'Go live' and focus on the organoid using the 4x objective. Focus on the brightest point of the organoid.
Search 'JOBs explorer' in the search bar in the top right corner of NIS elements.
Import the JOB into the JOBs explorer Select the JOB named 'Organoid_wellplate_3'
Run the JOB by clicking the button that looks like the 'play' symbol. This will open the JOB setup wizard where you can select imaging parameters.
Select the step size you would like to use for the high resolution Z-stacks. This is at the top of the setup wizard in the field named: 'Z Step Size?' and takes an integer.
Select the high magnification capture definition. This is where you'll input the 20x magnification optical configurations (OC) you made earlier.
Select the low magnification capture definition. This is where you'll input the 4x magnification optical configurations (OC) you made earlier.
Define the depth (in Z) and step size (in micrometers) you would like the microscope to search over when looking for organoids in each well. This is by default 500um range with 10um steps.
Select which wells you would like to image by clicking and dragging across the well area

Import the required general analysis 3 (GA3) recipes that work within the JOB to segment and save the XYZ locations of the organoids. You can do this by double clicking on the GA3 command in the JOB highlighted in red in the image below and then importing the GA3 recipes. The upper GA3 command should have the 'Z_abs_bounds_4x' GA3 recipe imported, and the lower GA3 command should have the 'Z_abs_bounds_20x' GA3 recipe imported.
creator: Joe Airey
